comment.body:
All signs point to Trump quietly backing away; investors don’t believe he can rein in the globalist power structure. But remember, if he somehow did, nearly every U.S. tariff would be rolled back overnight.
No meaningful deal is possible without concessions Ukraine and its European backers have already categorically rejected. The financial and military burden now falls heavily on EU members—Spain alone has committed €10.5 billion to meet NATO’s 2 percent-of-GDP defense benchmark, and Portugal is scrambling for fiscal wiggle room. That strain will soon become untenable.
In truth, London’s gathering is little more than a showpiece masking a renewed great-power rivalry.
It’s critical to follow today’s and this weekend’s developments in that broader context.
